author: codex-root

patch.value: Cross-project fleet finding: [private companion item pm-cli-website-ifph]. Prior partial fix/context: ops-f4vp. Public source remains isolated from hosted-service and user data.
Restore --repos routing for every fleet command
The pm CLI parser drops --repos values because all seven pm-ops flag definitions declare list:true without the required string value_type, silently falling back to the current directory.

All seven flag definitions satisfy the current pm SDK string-list contract; a parser-boundary regression test covers command registration; installed CLI tests prove supplied paths are used and cwd fallback is not; invalid paths remain explicit and safe; full release gate passes.
Install pm-ops into a clean pm project, run any ops subcommand with --repos pointing outside cwd, and observe the handler receives no repos option and scans cwd.

scan, policy, verify-release, report, status, outdated, and audit all preserve comma-separated and repeated --repos values through the real pm CLI parser; missing paths produce explicit per-repository diagnostics.
The real parser omits options.repos and resolveRepos falls back to process.cwd(); direct handler tests bypass the parser and therefore passed.
Agents can receive authoritative-looking fleet results for the wrong repository, breaking project-management-as-context-management decisions.

author: codex-root

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Black-box baseline proved pm ops status --repos <missing> exited 0 while reporting the clean fixture cwd instead. Adding value_type:string made pm contracts/help correct but the installed handler still received repos: []. Published host defect: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/550. Package workaround now uses the declared parser capability to recover only missing CLI --repos occurrences; structured SDK/MCP options remain authoritative and unchanged. Installed isolated tests cover all seven commands, one value, repeated values, comma lists, explicit missing-path diagnostics, and package doctor activation.

message: Record parser root cause, upstream issue, and compatibility strategy

author: codex-root

patch.value: All seven commands now declare typed repeatable repository flags and variadic additional-repository arguments. A parser compatibility bridge preserves CLI values on pm 2026.7.13 while leaving structured SDK/MCP options unchanged. Isolated install/package-doctor coverage, all CLI spellings, explicit missing paths, full 40-test real-data run, release gate, production audit, and package dry-run pass.
Restored repository scope through current SDK command, schema, and parser capabilities; added installed black-box regression coverage; filed the host coercion defect as pm-cli issue #550.

message: Record verified implementation and release target

patch.value: Verified all seven installed CLI routes, structured SDK preservation, real-repository behavior, and release readiness; upstream host defect tracked in pm-cli #550.

value.author: Codex
value.text: PR review round 2: Greptile P1 identified that a missing --repos value could be confused with a following long option in the compatibility scanner. The scanner now rejects separate values beginning with -- while preserving single-dash paths; double-dash-prefixed paths remain supported through --repos=--path. Installed CLI regression coverage proves --repos --json exits with structured invalid_command_usage instead of scanning cwd/--json.
